title: Missing NFS files in a podman run with a mount
body:
### Issue Description

This is the setup:

- AKS cluster in Azure
- File Share created in Azure and exposed inside AKS as a persistent volume
- Running podman container as a pod in the AKS cluster in privileged mode as root

The podman pod has 2 mounted volumes:
- A regular persistent volume with Disk as a backend under the path `/home/jovyan`
- The NFS persistent volume mounted at `/home/jovyan/shared`

When I run the command `podman run -v /home/jovyan:/home/jovyan docker.io/ubuntu ls -al /home/jovyan/shared`, I can see all the files in the NFS volume just fine inside of the container. However, when I add a uidmap `podman run --uidmap 0:2300:1 -v /home/jovyan:/home/jovyan docker.io/ubuntu ls -al /home/jovyan/shared`, none of the files from the NFS server appear there.

This is only happening in Azure, does not happen with EFS with EKS in AWS. So my thoughts are that there is some configuration needed for the NFS for this to work in AKS as well, however, it's not clear why it works without uidmap. So there's probably something happening inside Podman itself?

### Steps to reproduce the issue

Steps to reproduce the issue (only in Azure, I wasn't able to reproduce this locally)
1. Deploy AKS, File Share and create a persistent volume pointing to the File Share
2. Deploy a pod running podman with the mounts
3. Run the commands from the description


### Describe the results you received

Empty NFS folder

### Describe the results you expected

NFS folder with all the files present in the NFS

### podman info output

comments.body: A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.

Sorry but we have no way to debug such environments so I think you are own your own here.
You could check that the mount propagation is set correctly (`findmnt -o SOURCE,TARGET,PROPAGATION`) but I don't think this is related to the user namespace
